headlines.title: U.S. appeals court blocks President Trump's executive order suspending border asylum
headlines.summary: A three-judge panel of the U.S. Court of Appeals for the D.C. Circuit ruled Trump's order suspending asylum access illegal, siding with a lower court. Migrants at the southern border benefit while the administration's crackdown stalls. Supreme Court appeal likely follows. This halts a major migration policy pillar affecting U.S. immigration enforcement.

headlines.title: NASA and SpaceX launch Crew-10 rocket carrying four astronauts to retrieve two stuck on station
headlines.summary: SpaceX Falcon 9 launched Crew-10 from Kennedy Space Center at 7:03 p.m. Friday with NASA astronauts Anne McClain and Nichole Ayers, Japan's Takuya Onishi and Russia's Kirill Peskov to relieve Butch Wilmore and Suni Williams stuck nine months. Docking expected Saturday night. This enables the stuck crew's return, restoring ISS routine rotations vital for U.S. space program.

headlines.title: Justice Department authorizes firing squads to expedite federal capital punishment executions
headlines.summary: The Justice Department adopts firing squads and reauthorizes pentobarbital lethal injections to ramp up executions, following 13 prior uses. Death row inmates and states face faster federal cases. Implementation begins soon. This accelerates Trump administration's push for more executions amid debates on methods and rights.

headlines.title: U.S. Treasury sanctions China oil refinery and 40 shippers transporting Iranian petroleum
headlines.summary: The Trump administration targets a major Chinese refinery and about 40 shipping firms and tankers for Iranian oil trade, enforcing sanctions. Chinese firms, shippers and Iran lose revenue streams. Further actions possible. This pressures evasion networks, escalating U.S. campaign against Iranian oil exports amid tensions.

headlines.title: U.S. appeals court lifts block on Trump administration's crackdown against federal DEI programs
headlines.summary: Fourth Circuit panel in Richmond rules Trump's executive orders halting DEI support likely constitutional, pausing lower court block. Federal employees and agencies end DEI initiatives. Enforcement resumes immediately. This advances policy dismantling diversity programs, impacting workplace equity across U.S. government.

headlines.title: U.S. prosecutors indict Sinaloa governor Rubén Rocha Moya for cartel protection conspiracy
headlines.summary: Federal indictment accuses Rocha Moya and officials of yearslong plot shielding Sinaloa Cartel operations. Mexican officials, cartel members and U.S. anti-drug efforts affected. Extradition proceedings may follow. This exposes high-level corruption, straining U.S.-Mexico security ties and cartel disruptions.

headlines.title: Israel allows displaced Palestinians to cross zone back into northern Gaza after hostage breakthrough
headlines.summary: Israel permits Gazans to return north after resolving hostage release deadlock bisecting the enclave. Over one million displaced residents regain access. Further movements depend on cease-fire talks. This eases humanitarian strain in war-torn Gaza, signaling potential de-escalation steps.

headlines.title: Rwanda-backed rebels capture Congo safe-haven city Goma displacing over one million residents
headlines.summary: Rebels overrun Congolese troops entering Goma amid gunfire and shelling, trapping more than one million displaced in the aid hub. Eastern Congo civilians face intensified conflict. U.N. evacuation plans advance. This threatens regional stability and humanitarian crisis in Africa's deadliest war zone.

headlines.title: NATO deploys first coordinated response to suspected sabotage severing Baltic Sea cable
headlines.summary: Alliance activates response after deep-sea cable cut in suspected sabotage campaign against infrastructure. Baltic nations and NATO allies secure networks. Investigations pinpoint culprits. This marks escalated defense of critical undersea assets amid hybrid threats from Russia.

headlines.title: Defense Secretary Hegseth spars with lawmakers for six hours over Iran war strategy
headlines.summary: Pete Hegseth faces Democratic questions under oath in first hearing since Iran war began. U.S. Congress and military policymakers debate conduct. Further oversight hearings scheduled. This highlights tensions in war oversight, influencing U.S. policy and funding amid ongoing conflict.
